# Large Test CLAUDE.md

## Architecture

This project follows a layered architecture with clear separation between the presentation layer, business logic layer, and data access layer. Each layer communicates through well-defined interfaces and dependency injection patterns.

## Code Conventions

All code must follow the team style guide. Functions should be pure where possible. Side effects must be explicitly documented. Async operations must be properly awaited and errors must be caught at appropriate boundaries.

## Security Guidelines

Never expose API keys in code. Always validate user input at the boundary. Use parameterized queries for all database access. Sanitize all user-generated content before rendering. Apply principle of least privilege for all service accounts.

## Testing Standards

All new features require unit tests with >80% coverage. Integration tests must cover the happy path and at least two error scenarios. Mock all external dependencies in unit tests. Use real database connections in integration tests.

## Deployment Process

All deployments go through the CI/CD pipeline. No direct pushes to main. Every PR requires two reviewer approvals. Staging must pass smoke tests before production promotion. Rollback plan must be documented for breaking changes.

## Database Conventions

Use migrations for all schema changes. Never modify existing migrations. Index all foreign keys and frequently queried columns. Use transactions for multi-step operations. Document all table and column purposes.

## API Design

Follow REST conventions. Use semantic HTTP status codes. Version all APIs. Document with OpenAPI/Swagger. Rate limiting must be configured. Authentication required on all non-public endpoints.

## Performance Guidelines

N+1 queries are forbidden. Paginate all list endpoints. Cache expensive computations. Profile before optimizing. Document performance-critical paths. Use connection pooling for database access.
